description.value: The Contributorship Resource enables reference to any knowledge artifact for purposes of attribution. The Contributorship Resource describes any aspect of contributorship to a journal article, report, document or resource. Contributorship data are used extensively in scholarly communication and important for documenting the evidence or source behind statements of fact, opinion and interpretation. Contributorship data are also used for acknowledgement of the contributions of individuals and groups to the creation and advancement of knowledge.

short.value: A description of the contributorship to a knowledge object (article or artifact)

definition.value: The Contributorship Resource enables reference to any knowledge artifact for purposes of attribution. The Contributorship Resource describes any aspect of contributorship to a journal article, report, document or resource. Contributorship data are used extensively in scholarly communication and important for documenting the evidence or source behind statements of fact, opinion and interpretation. Contributorship data are also used for acknowledgement of the contributions of individuals and groups to the creation and advancement of knowledge.

human.value: Name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation

short.value: Logical id of this artifact

definition.value: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

comment.value: Within the context of the FHIR RESTful interactions, the resource has an id except for cases like the create and conditional update. Otherwise, the use of the resouce id depends on the given use case.

short.value: Metadata about the resource

definition.value: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: A set of rules under which this content was created

definition.value: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

comment.value: Asserting this rule set restricts the content to be only understood by a limited set of trading partners. This inherently limits the usefulness of the data in the long term. However, the existing health eco-system is highly fractured, and not yet ready to define, collect, and exchange data in a generally computable sense. Wherever possible, implementers and/or specification writers should avoid using this element. Often, when used, the URL is a reference to an implementation guide that defines these special rules as part of its narrative along with other profiles, value sets, etc.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

isModifierReason.value: This element is labeled as a modifier because the implicit rules may provide additional knowledge about the resource that modifies its meaning or interpretation

short.value: Language of the resource content

definition.value: The base language in which the resource is written.

comment.value: Language is provided to support indexing and accessibility (typically, services such as text to speech use the language tag). The html language tag in the narrative applies to the narrative. The language tag on the resource may be used to specify the language of other presentations generated from the data in the resource. Not all the content has to be in the base language. The Resource.language should not be assumed to apply to the narrative automatically. If a language is specified, it should it also be specified on the div element in the html (see rules in HTML5 for information about the relationship between xml:lang and the html lang attribute).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

description.value: IETF language tag for a human language

short.value: Text summary of the resource, for human interpretation

definition.value: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

comment.value: Contained resources do not have a narrative. Resources that are not contained SHOULD have a narrative. In some cases, a resource may only have text with little or no additional discrete data (as long as all minOccurs=1 elements are satisfied). This may be necessary for data from legacy systems where information is captured as a "text blob" or where text is additionally entered raw or narrated and encoded information is added later.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Contained, inline Resources

definition.value: These resources do not have an independent existence apart from the resource that contains them - they cannot be identified independently, nor can they have their own independent transaction scope. This is allowed to be a Parameters resource if and only if it is referenced by a resource that provides context/meaning.

comment.value: This should never be done when the content can be identified properly, as once identification is lost, it is extremely difficult (and context dependent) to restore it again. Contained resources may have profiles and tags in their meta elements, but SHALL NOT have security labels. Contained resources may be a resource type defined in the FHIR specification, or an [additional resource](resource.html#additional).

short.value: Additional content defined by implementations

definition.value: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource and that modifies the understanding of the element that contains it and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer is allowed to define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the resource that contains them

short.value: Canonical identifier for this contributorship record, represented as a globally unique URI

definition.value: An absolute URI that is used to identify this contributorship record when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which an authoritative instance of this summary is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the summary is stored on different servers.

comment.value: In some cases, the resource can no longer be found at the stated url, but the url itself cannot change. Implementations can use the meta.source element to indicate where the current master source of the resource can be found.

requirements.value: Allows the contributorship record to be referenced by a single globally unique identifier.

human.value: URL should not contain | or # - these characters make processing canonical references problematic
All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Identifier for the contributorship record itself

definition.value: A formal identifier that is used to identify this contributorship record when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

comment.value: Use this element if you need to identify the contributorship record independently from identifying the referenced artifact.

requirements.value: Allows externally provided and/or usable identifiers to be associated with this contributorship record.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Business version of the contributorship record

definition.value: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the contributorship record when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the contributorship record author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence.

comment.value: This is a business version label used for display in understanding the formal version of the contributorship record, not a resource version id or technical version number for an archival system. There may be different contributorship record instances that have the same identifier but different versions. The version can be appended to the url in a reference to allow a reference to a particular business version of the contributorship record with the format [url]|[version]. The version SHOULD NOT contain a '#' - see [Business Version](resource.html#bv-format).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: How to compare versions

definition.value: Indicates the mechanism used to compare versions to determine which is more current.

comment.value: If set as a string, this is a FHIRPath expression that has two additional context variables passed in - %version1 and %version2 and will return a negative number if version1 is newer, a positive number if version2 is newer, and a 0 if the version ordering can't successfully be determined.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Name for this contributorship record (computer friendly)

definition.value: A natural language name identifying the contributorship record. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

comment.value: The name is not expected to be globally unique. The name should be a simple alphanumeric type name to ensure that it is machine-processing friendly.

requirements.value: Support human navigation and code generation.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Name for this contributorship record (human friendly)

definition.value: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the contributorship record.

comment.value: This name does not need to be machine-processing friendly and may contain punctuation, white-space, etc. Use this element if you have a need for naming the contributorship record for human use, such as a display name for the contributorship record that is used when selecting contributorship records from a list.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: draft | active | retired | unknown

definition.value: The status of this summary. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

comment.value:
Allows filtering of summaries that are appropriate for use versus not. Use "active" when the contributorship record is the version for others to actively use, "draft" while you are developing the content, and "retired" when this record should no longer be used for any purpose.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](canonicalresource.html#loca lization).

requirements.value: Allows filtering of contributorship records that are appropriate for use versus not.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

isModifierReason.value: This is labeled as "Is Modifier" because applications should not use a retired {{title}} without due consideration

short.value: For testing only - never for real usage

definition.value: A Boolean value to indicate that this contributorship record is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and no version of this resource will ever be intended for genuine usage.

comment.value:
Allows filtering of contributorship records that are appropriate for use versus not. Experimental resources might include example instances in implementation guides, instances created solely for testing purposes, etc. If experimental is 'true' for any version of a resource, it should be true for all versions of the resource. (If experimental changes, then it is being misused or a resource that was never intended for real-world use has unexpectedly changed its purpose.).
Experimental resources are not expected to be stable and may well have breaking changes without a corresponding change to the 'version' element.

requirements.value: Enables experimental content to be developed following the same lifecycle that would be used for a production-level contributorship record.

meaningWhenMissing.value: If absent, this resource is treated as though it is not experimental.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Date last changed

definition.value: The date (and optionally time) when the contributorship record was last significantly changed. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the contributorship record changes.

comment.value:
The date is often not tracked until the resource is published, but may be present on draft content. Note that this is not the same as the resource last-modified-date, since the resource may be a secondary representation of the contributorship record. Additional specific dates may be added as extensions or be found by consulting Provenances associated with past versions of the resource.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](canonicalresource.html#loca lization).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Who authored the contributorship record

definition.value: Who authored or created the contributorship record.

comment.value: The Contributorship.author element is structured to support a name (with a string datatype) and contact point information for the author/creator, and is not structured to support unique identifiers for the author.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: The publisher of the contributorship record, not the publisher of the article or artifact being cited

definition.value: The name of the organization or individual that published the contributorship record.

comment.value: Usually an organization but may be an individual. The publisher (or steward) of the contributorship record is the organization or individual primarily responsible for the maintenance and upkeep of the contributorship record. This is not necessarily the same individual or organization that developed and initially authored the content. The publisher is the primary point of contact for questions or issues with the contributorship record. This item SHOULD be populated unless the information is available from context.

requirements.value: Helps establish the "authority/credibility" of the contributorship record. May also allow for contact.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Contact details for the publisher of the contributorship record

definition.value: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

comment.value:
May be a web site, an email address, a telephone number, etc.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](canonicalresource.html#loca lization).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Natural language description of the contributorship

definition.value: A free text natural language description of the contributorship from a consumer's perspective.

comment.value: This description can be used to capture details such as comments about misuse, instructions for clinical use and interpretation, literature references, examples from the paper world, etc. It is not a rendering of the contributorship as conveyed in the 'text' field of the resource itself. This item SHOULD be populated unless the information is available from context (e.g. the language of the contributorship is presumed to be the predominant language in the place the contributorship was created).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: The context that the contributorship record content is intended to support

definition.value: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate contributorship record instances.

comment.value: When multiple useContexts are specified, there is no expectation that all or any of the contexts apply.

requirements.value: Assist in searching for appropriate content.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Why this contributorship is defined

definition.value: Explanation of why this contributorship is needed and why it has been designed as it has.

comment.value: This element does not describe the usage of the contributorship. Instead, it provides traceability of ''why'' the resource is either needed or ''why'' it is defined as it is. This may be used to point to source materials or specifications that drove the structure of this contributorship.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Use and/or publishing restrictions for the contributorship record, not for the referenced artifact

definition.value: Use and/or publishing restrictions for the contributorship record, not for the referenced artifact.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Copyright holder and year(s) for the ciation record, not for the cited artifact

definition.value: A short string (<50 characters), suitable for inclusion in a page footer that identifies the copyright holder, effective period, and optionally whether rights are resctricted. (e.g. 'All rights reserved', 'Some rights reserved').

comment.value: The (c) symbol should NOT be included in this string. It will be added by software when rendering the notation. Full details about licensing, restrictions, warrantees, etc. goes in the more general 'copyright' element.

requirements.value: Defines the content expected to be rendered in all representations of the artifact.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: When the contributorship record was approved by publisher

definition.value: The date on which the resource content was approved by the publisher. Approval happens once when the content is officially approved for usage.

comment.value:
The 'date' element may be more recent than the approval date because of minor changes or editorial corrections.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](canonicalresource.html#loca lization).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: When the contributorship record was last reviewed by the publisher

definition.value: The date on which the resource content was last reviewed. Review happens periodically after approval but does not change the original approval date.

comment.value:
If specified, this date follows the original approval date.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](canonicalresource.html#loca lization).

requirements.value: Gives a sense of how "current" the content is. Resources that have not been reviewed in a long time may have a risk of being less appropriate/relevant.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: When the contributorship record is expected to be used

definition.value: The period during which the contributorship record content was or is planned to be in active use.

comment.value:
The effective period for a contributorship record determines when the content is applicable for usage and is independent of publication and review dates.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](canonicalresource.html#loca lization).

requirements.value: Allows establishing a transition before a resource comes into effect and also allows for a sunsetting process when new versions of the contributorship record are or are expected to be used instead.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Who entered the data for the contributorship record

definition.value: Who entered the data for the contributorship record.

comment.value: The Contributorship.recorder element is structured to support a name (with a string datatype) and contact point information for the recorder, and is not structured to support unique identifiers for the recorder.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Who edited the contributorship record

definition.value: Who edited or revised the contributorship record.

comment.value: The Contributorship.editor element is structured to support a name (with a string datatype) and contact point information for the editor, and is not structured to support unique identifiers for the editor.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Who reviewed the contributorship record

definition.value: Who reviewed the contributorship record.

comment.value:
The Contributorship.reviewer element is structured to support a name (with a string datatype) and contact point information for the reviewer, and is not structured to support unique identifiers for the reviewer.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](canonicalresource.html#loca lization).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Who endorsed the contributorship record

definition.value: Who endorsed the contributorship record.

comment.value:
The Contributorship.endorser element is structured to support a name (with a string datatype) and contact point information for the endorser, and is not structured to support unique identifiers for the endorser.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](canonicalresource.html#loca lization).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Used for general notes and annotations not coded elsewhere

definition.value: Used for general notes and annotations not coded elsewhere.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: The artifact that is the subject of this Contributorship

definition.value: The artifact that is the subject of this Contributorship Resource.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Relationships to other Resources

definition.value: Relationships that this Contributorship has with other FHIR or non-FHIR resources that already exist.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children or both

short.value: Unique id for inter-element referencing

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

short.value: Additional content defined by implementations

definition.value: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

short.value: documentation | justification | predecessor | successor | derived-from | depends-on | composed-of | part-of | amends | amended-with | appends | appended-with | cites | cited-by | comments-on | comment-in | contains | contained-in | corrects | correction-in | replaces | replaced-with | retracts | retracted-by | signs | similar-to | supports | supported-with | transforms | transformed-into | transformed-with | specification-of | created-with | cite-as | summarizes

definition.value: The type of relationship to the related artifact.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

description.value: The type of relationship to the related artifact.

short.value: The artifact that is related to this Contributorship

definition.value: The artifact that is related to this Contributorship Resource.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Indicates if all authors are included

definition.value: Indication whether all authors are included in the entry content.

comment.value: If list is incomplete, "et al" can be appended to Contributorship.summary.value for display purposes.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: An individual entity named as a contributor

definition.value: An individual entity named as a contributor, for example in the author list or contributor list.

comment.value: Used to report contributorship in individualized ways.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children or both

short.value: Unique id for inter-element referencing

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

short.value: Additional content defined by implementations

definition.value: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

short.value: The identity of the individual contributor

definition.value: The identity of the individual contributor.

comment.value: If naming a contributor without additional detail or further structured data, the display element within the Reference datatype may be used for a simple string without referencing another resource.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: For citation styles that use initials

definition.value: For citation styles that use initials.

comment.value: This element is used to support a data element used for citations that is not otherwise supported fully by FHIR HumanName datatype. In some citation summary styles (e.g. Medline), authors are presented with full spelling of their last name (family name) and initials for all forenames (first names, middle names, preceding names, given names). As the FHIR HumanName Datatype does not have a specific element for forename initials and this is common in citation usage, the element is added to Contributorship.entry directly.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Organizational affiliation

definition.value: Organization affiliated with the contributor.

comment.value: If naming an organization without additional detail or further structured data, the display element within the Reference datatype may be used for a simple string without referencing another resource.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: The specific contribution

definition.value: The specific nature of an individual’s contribution with respect to the cited work.

comment.value: The CreDiT taxonomy provides a way to encode contribution information. The purpose of CRediT is to provide transparency in contributions to scholarly published work, to enable improved systems of attribution, credit, and accountability.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: The role of the contributor (e.g. author, editor, reviewer, funder)

definition.value: The role of the contributor (e.g. author, editor, reviewer, funder).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Contributions with accounting for time or number

definition.value: Contributions with accounting for time or number.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children or both

short.value: Unique id for inter-element referencing

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

short.value: Additional content defined by implementations

definition.value: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

short.value: The specific contribution

definition.value: The specific contribution.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: The time that the contribution was made

definition.value: The time that the contribution was made.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Whether the contributor is the corresponding contributor for the role

definition.value: Whether the contributor is the corresponding contributor for the role.

comment.value: If there are multiple authors and one serves as the corresponding author, then that one would have correspondingContact = true.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Ranked order of contribution

definition.value: Provides a numerical ranking to represent the degree of contributorship relative to other contributors, such as 1 for first author and 2 for second author.

comment.value: Used to retain order of authorship as structured data

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Used to record a display of the author/contributor list without separate data element for each list member

definition.value: Used to record a display of the author/contributor list without separate data element for each list member.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children or both

short.value: Unique id for inter-element referencing

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

short.value: Additional content defined by implementations

definition.value: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

short.value: Such as author list, contributorship statement, funding statement, acknowledgements statement, or conflicts of interest statement

definition.value: Used most commonly to express an author list or a contributorship statement.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: The format for the display string

definition.value: The format for the display string, such as author last name with first letter capitalized followed by forename initials.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Used to code the producer or rule for creating the display string

definition.value: Used to code the producer or rule for creating the display string.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: The display string for the author list, contributor list, or contributorship statement

definition.value: The display string for the author list, contributor list, or contributorship statement.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Attribution of authors and other contributors

definition.value: This element is used to list authors and other contributors, their contact information, specific contributions, and summary statements.

requirements.value: Need to support individualized and collective attribution of authorship and contributorship to people and groups.

short.value: Indicates if all authors are included

definition.value: Indication whether all authors are included in the entry content.

comment.value: If list is incomplete, "et al" can be appended to Contributorship.summary.value for display purposes.

short.value: An individual entity named as a contributor

definition.value: An individual entity named as a contributor, for example in the author list or contributor list.

comment.value: Used to report contributorship in individualized ways.

short.value: The identity of the individual contributor

comment.value: If naming a contributor without additional detail or further structured data, the display element within the Reference datatype may be used for a simple string without referencing another resource.

short.value: For citation styles that use initials

comment.value: This element is used to support a data element used for citations that is not otherwise supported fully by FHIR HumanName datatype. In some citation summary styles (e.g. Medline), authors are presented with full spelling of their last name (family name) and initials for all forenames (first names, middle names, preceding names, given names). As the FHIR HumanName Datatype does not have a specific element for forename initials and this is common in citation usage, the element is added to contributorship.entry directly.

short.value: Organizational affiliation

definition.value: Organization affiliated with the contributor.

comment.value: If naming an organization without additional detail or further structured data, the display element within the Reference datatype may be used for a simple string without referencing another resource.

short.value: The specific contribution

definition.value: The specific nature of an individual’s contribution with respect to the cited work.

comment.value: The CreDiT taxonomy provides a way to encode contribution information. The purpose of CRediT is to provide transparency in contributions to scholarly published work, to enable improved systems of attribution, credit, and accountability.

short.value: The role of the contributor (e.g. author, editor, reviewer, funder)
Contributions with accounting for time or number
The specific contribution
The time that the contribution was made
Whether the contributor is the corresponding contributor for the role

comment.value: If there are multiple authors and one serves as the corresponding author, then that one would have correspondingContact = true.

short.value: Ranked order of contribution

definition.value: Provides a numerical ranking to represent the degree of contributorship relative to other contributors, such as 1 for first author and 2 for second author.

comment.value: Used to retain order of authorship as structured data

short.value: Used to record a display of the author/contributor list without separate data element for each list member
Such as author list, contributorship statement, funding statement, acknowledgements statement, or conflicts of interest statement

definition.value: Used most commonly to express an author list or a contributorship statement.

short.value: The format for the display string

definition.value: The format for the display string, such as author last name with first letter capitalized followed by forename initials.

short.value: Used to code the producer or rule for creating the display string
The display string for the author list, contributor list, or contributorship statement
A description of contributorship of a publication (article or artifact)

definition.value: The Contributorship Resource enables reference to any knowledge artifact for purposes of attribution.

human.value: Name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation

short.value: Canonical identifier for this contributorship record, represented as a globally unique URI

definition.value: An absolute URI that is used to identify this contributorship record when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which an authoritative instance of this summary is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the summary is stored on different servers.

comment.value: In some cases, the resource can no longer be found at the stated url, but the url itself cannot change. Implementations can use the meta.source element to indicate where the current master source of the resource can be found.

requirements.value: Allows the contributorship record to be referenced by a single globally unique identifier.

human.value: URL should not contain | or # - these characters make processing canonical references problematic

short.value: Identifier for the contributorship record itself

definition.value: A formal identifier that is used to identify this contributorship record when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

comment.value: Use this element if you need to identify the contributorship record independently from identifying the cited artifact.

requirements.value: Allows externally provided and/or usable identifiers to be associated with this contributorship record.

short.value: Business version of the contributorship record

definition.value: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the contributorship record when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the contributorship record author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence.

comment.value: This is a business version label used for display in understanding the formal version of the contributorship record, not a resource version id or technical version number for an archival system. There may be different contributorship record instances that have the same identifier but different versions. The version can be appended to the url in a reference to allow a reference to a particular business version of the contributorship record with the format [url]|[version]. The version SHOULD NOT contain a '#' - see [Business Version](resource.html#bv-format).

short.value: How to compare versions

definition.value: Indicates the mechanism used to compare versions to determine which is more current.

comment.value: If set as a string, this is a FHIRPath expression that has two additional context variables passed in - %version1 and %version2 and will return a negative number if version1 is newer, a positive number if version2 is newer, and a 0 if the version ordering can't successfully be determined.

short.value: Name for this contributorship record (computer friendly)

definition.value: A natural language name identifying the contributorship record. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

comment.value: The name is not expected to be globally unique. The name should be a simple alphanumeric type name to ensure that it is machine-processing friendly.

requirements.value: Support human navigation and code generation.

short.value: Name for this contributorship record (human friendly)

definition.value: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the contributorship record.

comment.value: This name does not need to be machine-processing friendly and may contain punctuation, white-space, etc. Use this element if you have a need for naming the contributorship record for human use, such as a display name for the contributorship record that is used when selecting contributorships from a list.

short.value: draft | active | retired | unknown

definition.value: The status of this summary. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

comment.value:
Allows filtering of summaries that are appropriate for use versus not. Use "active" when the contributorship record is the version for others to actively use, "draft" while you are developing the content, and "retired" when this record should no longer be used for any purpose.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](canonicalresource.html#localization).

requirements.value: Allows filtering of contributorship records that are appropriate for use versus not.

isModifierReason.value: This is labeled as "Is Modifier" because applications should not use a retired {{title}} without due consideration

short.value: For testing only - never for real usage

definition.value: A Boolean value to indicate that this contributorship record is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and no version of this resource will ever be intended for genuine usage.

comment.value:
Allows filtering of contributorship records that are appropriate for use versus not.. Experimental resources might include example instances in implementation guides, instances created solely for testing purposes, etc. If experimental is 'true' for any version of a resource, it should be true for all versions of the resource. (If experimental changes, then it is being misused or a resource that was never intended for real-world use has unexpectedly changed its purpose.).
Experimental resources are not expected to be stable and may well have breaking changes without a corresponding change to the 'version' element.

requirements.value: Enables experimental content to be developed following the same lifecycle that would be used for a production-level contributorship record.

meaningWhenMissing.value: If absent, this resource is treated as though it is not experimental.

short.value: Date last changed

definition.value: The date (and optionally time) when the contributorship record was last significantly changed. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the contributorship record changes.

comment.value:
The date is often not tracked until the resource is published, but may be present on draft content. Note that this is not the same as the resource last-modified-date, since the resource may be a secondary representation of the contributorship record. Additional specific dates may be added as extensions or be found by consulting Provenances associated with past versions of the resource.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](canonicalresource.html#localization).

short.value: Who authored the contributorship record

definition.value: Who authored or created the contributorship record.

comment.value: The Contributorship.author element is structured to support a name (with a string datatype) and contact point information for the author/creator, and is not structured to support unique identifiers for the author. If detailed contributorship data is needed for the authorship of the contributorship record, then one can create a Contributorship Resource for the Contributorship Resource.

short.value: The publisher of the contributorship record, not the publisher of the article or artifact being cited

definition.value: The name of the organization or individual that published the contributorship record.

comment.value: Usually an organization but may be an individual. The publisher (or steward) of the contributorship record is the organization or individual primarily responsible for the maintenance and upkeep of the contributorship record. This is not necessarily the same individual or organization that developed and initially authored the content. The publisher is the primary point of contact for questions or issues with the contributorship record. This item SHOULD be populated unless the information is available from context.

requirements.value: Helps establish the "authority/credibility" of the contributorship record. May also allow for contact.

short.value: Contact details for the publisher of the contributorship record

definition.value: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

comment.value:
May be a web site, an email address, a telephone number, etc.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](canonicalresource.html#localization).

short.value: Natural language description of the contributorship

definition.value: A free text natural language description of the contributorship from a consumer's perspective.

comment.value: This description can be used to capture details such as comments about misuse, instructions for clinical use and interpretation, literature references, examples from the paper world, etc. It is not a rendering of the contributorship as conveyed in the 'text' field of the resource itself. This item SHOULD be populated unless the information is available from context (e.g. the language of the contributorship is presumed to be the predominant language in the place the contributorship was created).

short.value: The context that the contributorship record content is intended to support

definition.value: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate contributorship record instances.

comment.value: When multiple useContexts are specified, there is no expectation that all or any of the contexts apply.

requirements.value: Assist in searching for appropriate content.

short.value: Jurisdiction of the authority that maintains the contributorship record (if applicable)

definition.value: A legal or geographic region in which the authority that maintains the resource is operating. In general, the jurisdiction is also found in the useContext. The useContext may reference additional jurisdictions because the defining jurisdiction does not necessarily limit the jurisdictions of use.

comment.value:
It may be possible for the contributorship record to be used in jurisdictions other than those for which it was originally designed or intended.
DEPRECATION NOTE: For consistency, implementations are encouraged to migrate to using the new 'jurisdiction' code in the useContext element. (I.e. useContext.code indicating http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/usage-context-type#jurisdiction and useContext.valueCodeableConcept indicating the jurisdiction.)

short.value: Why this contributorship is defined

definition.value: Explanation of why this contributorship is needed and why it has been designed as it has.

comment.value: This element does not describe the usage of the contributorship. Instead, it provides traceability of ''why'' the resource is either needed or ''why'' it is defined as it is. This may be used to point to source materials or specifications that drove the structure of this contributorship.

short.value: Notice about intellectual property ownership, can include restrictions on use for the contributorship record, not for the cited artifact
Copyright holder and year(s) for the ciation record, not for the cited artifact

definition.value: A short string (<50 characters), suitable for inclusion in a page footer that identifies the copyright holder, effective period, and optionally whether rights are restricted. (e.g. 'All rights reserved', 'Some rights reserved').

comment.value: The copyright symbol and the '(c)' textual representation SHOULD NOT be included in this string. It will be added by software when rendering the notation. Full details about licensing, restrictions, warrantees, etc. goes in the more general 'copyright' element.

requirements.value: Defines the content expected to be rendered in all representations of the artifact.

short.value: When the contributorship record was approved by publisher

definition.value: The date on which the resource content was approved by the publisher. Approval happens once when the content is officially approved for usage.

comment.value:
The 'date' element may be more recent than the approval date because of minor changes or editorial corrections.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](canonicalresource.html#localization).

short.value: When the contributorship record was last reviewed by the publisher

definition.value: The date on which the resource content was last reviewed. Review happens periodically after approval but does not change the original approval date.

comment.value:
If specified, this date follows the original approval date.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](canonicalresource.html#localization).

requirements.value: Gives a sense of how "current" the content is. Resources that have not been reviewed in a long time may have a risk of being less appropriate/relevant.

short.value: When the contributorship record is expected to be used

definition.value: The period during which the contributorship record content was or is planned to be in active use.

comment.value:
The effective period for a contributorship record determines when the content is applicable for usage and is independent of publication and review dates. For example, a contributorship intended to be used for the year 2016 might be published in 2015.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](canonicalresource.html#localization).

requirements.value: Allows establishing a transition before a resource comes into effect and also allows for a sunsetting process when new versions of the contributorship record are or are expected to be used instead.

short.value: Who entered the data for the contributorship record

definition.value: Who entered the data for the contributorship record.

comment.value: The Contributorship.recorder element is structured to support a name (with a string datatype) and contact point information for the recorder, and is not structured to support unique identifiers for the recorder. If detailed contributorship data is needed, then one can create a Contributorship Resource for the Contributorship Resource.

short.value: Who edited the contributorship record

definition.value: Who edited or revised the contributorship record.

comment.value: The Contributorship.editor element is structured to support a name (with a string datatype) and contact point information for the editor, and is not structured to support unique identifiers for the editor. If detailed contributorship data is needed for the editing of the contributorship record, then one can create a Contributorship Resource for the Contributorship Resource.

short.value: Who reviewed the contributorship record

comment.value:
The Contributorship.reviewer element is structured to support a name (with a string datatype) and contact point information for the reviewer, and is not structured to support unique identifiers for the reviewer. If detailed contributorship data is needed for the review of the contributorship record, then one can create a Contributorship Resource for the Contributorship Resource.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](canonicalresource.html#localization).

short.value: Who endorsed the contributorship record

comment.value:
The Contributorship.endorser element is structured to support a name (with a string datatype) and contact point information for the endorser, and is not structured to support unique identifiers for the endorser. If detailed contributorship data is needed for the endorsement of the contributorship record, then one can create a Contributorship Resource for the Contributorship Resource.
See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](canonicalresource.html#localization).

short.value: Used for general notes and annotations not coded elsewhere
Relationships to other Resources

definition.value: Relationships that this Contributorship has with other FHIR or non-FHIR resources that already exist.

short.value: documentation | justification | citation | predecessor | successor | derived-from | depends-on | composed-of | part-of | amends | amended-with | appends | appended-with | cites | cited-by | comments-on | comment-in | contains | contained-in | corrects | correction-in | replaces | replaced-with | retracts | retracted-by | signs | similar-to | supports | supported-with | transforms | transformed-into | transformed-with | documents | specification-of | created-with | cite-as | reprint | reprint-of | summarizes

definition.value: The type of relationship to the related artifact.

description.value: The type of relationship to the related artifact.

short.value: The artifact that is related to this Contributorship

definition.value: The artifact that is related to this Contributorship Resource.
